message:
test: add 54 input validation + serde tests for tool modules

Add comprehensive unit tests for tool input validation and response type
serialization across identity, contacts, messaging, and reservations modules.

- identity.rs (21 tests): redact_database_url, attachments_policy validation,
  response type serialization, path/agent-name/program-model validation
- contacts.rs (12 tests): normalize_contact_policy, response type serde,
  TTL defaults
- messaging.rs (9 new tests): empty recipients, InboxMessage skip_serializing,
  ReadStatus/AckStatus/MessagePayload/ReplyMessage serde
- reservations.rs (12 new tests): empty paths, TTL edge cases,
  GrantedReservation/ReservationConflict/Release/Renewal serde

Extract is_valid_attachments_policy() and normalize_contact_policy() as
public testable helpers (deduplicate inline validation logic).

Close br-1m6a and br-1m6a.22 (all console enhancement work complete).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-1bm.8","title":"Bearer Auth Parity","description":"Objective:
Ensure Bearer token auth middleware behavior matches legacy Python, including localhost bypass logic and forwarded-header safeguards.

Legacy Reference Behavior:
- If bearer token configured, require Authorization: Bearer <token>
- If allow_localhost_unauthenticated and client is localhost (no forwarded headers), bypass Authorization
- OPTIONS and /health/* always allowed
- Constant-time comparison for token equality

Implementation Plan:
- Verify Rust middleware path covers OPTIONS + /health/* bypass.
- Enforce constant-time token compare.
- Use peer_addr + forwarded headers for localhost detection (`br-1bm.3`).

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
- Unit tests for token comparison + bypass conditions.
- Integration tests for /health endpoints, OPTIONS, and standard POSTs.
- E2E coverage via `br-2ei.9.6` (HTTP parity suite) including forwarded-header negative cases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Responses and bypass behavior match legacy exactly (status + JSON body).
- Constant-time compare is used (no early-return string compare).
- E2E artifacts include explicit allow/deny traces and header diffs on mismatch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDune","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:12.124733018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:03:49.815525779Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:03:49.815475775Z","close_reason":"Implemented legacy bearer auth parity + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-1bm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:12.124733018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:23.276913856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:08:56.484645140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- JSON payloads and status codes identical to legacy for all endpoints.

 {"id":"br-1e5h","title":"DB concurrency parity: backoff + circuit breaker","description":"## Objective
Implement database lock contention handling and circuit breaker per legacy concurrency model.

## Scope
- Exponential backoff with jitter on SQLITE_BUSY/locked errors.
- Circuit breaker: after 5 consecutive failures, open for 30s (fail fast with `DATABASE_POOL_EXHAUSTED`/`RESOURCE_BUSY`).
- Connection pooling defaults: base 3 + overflow 4, recycle 30min, busy timeout 5s.

## Tests
- Unit tests simulating lock contention and backoff sequence.
- Integration tests for circuit breaker open/close behavior.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store backoff/circuit logs under `tests/artifacts/db/concurrency/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Backoff and circuit breaker match legacy thresholds and timings.
2. Errors are mapped to legacy error codes.
3. Tests are deterministic and do not rely on real contention.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:50.675607847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:21:45.705741026Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:21:45.705722471Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1e5h","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:55.369503695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-1m6a","title":"[epic] Dramatically enhance console output with frankentui","description":"Master epic for dramatically improving the MCP Agent Mail server console UX using FrankenTUI.

BACKGROUND
- Legacy Python has rich_logger.py: highly structured Rich panels for startup, tool calls, and key events.
- Rust server already uses FrankenTUI inline mode (TerminalWriter) for a persistent HUD while preserving scrollback.

PRIMARY GOALS
- Python parity: replicate the important *information architecture* of rich_logger.py (startup banner sections, tool call start/end panels, query stats, request panels).
- FrankenTUI-native upgrades: keep a stable inline HUD, add sparklines, toast notifications, subtle animations, and better discoverability (links, tips, command palette) without sacrificing determinism.
- User-tunable console: interactive layout configuration (bottom % vs left split) and theme selection, persisted to a user config file so it "just remembers" (enabled by default in real TTYs).
- Safety by default: never leak secrets in panels; mask sensitive values; keep output bounded (truncate huge JSON).
- Zero-surprise behavior: when rich output is disabled or stdout is not a TTY, fall back to plain, grep-friendly text with no giant ANSI art dumps.
- Performance: when tool logging is disabled, overhead must be near-zero (no JSON pretty-printing, no allocations on hot path).

SCOPE
- Server console only (primarily `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server`).
- Includes both HTTP mode and stdio mode behavior where it impacts console output.

KEY CONSTRAINTS
- Inline mode must preserve scrollback and not corrupt the HUD.
- Output must remain deterministic enough for unit tests and E2E scripts.

IMPLEMENTATION DIRECTION (PREFERRED)
- Prefer rendering rich panels via ftui widgets into an offscreen `ftui::Buffer` + `ftui::Frame`, then exporting to ANSI using `ftui-extras` (TextExporter). This avoids brittle manual width/ANSI bookkeeping.
- Centralize styling on FrankenTUI's theme system (no ad-hoc ANSI constants).
- Centralize masking + safe JSON formatting utilities.

DONE WHEN
- Startup banner matches parity sections and is pleasant in real terminals.
- Tool call start/end panels exist with syntax-highlighted JSON and per-call query stats.
- Request panels are color-coded and consistent with theme.
- HUD shows useful live telemetry (counts, req/s trends, toasts).
- Unit tests + new console E2E suite pass and are stable in CI.","notes":"User requirements recap (as of 2026-02-07)
- Rich/TUI console output must be enabled by default in real TTYs (`LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` default).
- Console layout must be interactively tunable (HUD height %, anchor, inline auto-size, left split ratio) and automatically remembered via `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH` (default `~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env`).
- Interactive tuning must never interfere with stdio MCP protocol (so interactive input stays HTTP-only).

Plan-space guardrails
- Any new interactive UX should ship with:
  - pure unit tests for state transitions (no PTY needed)
  - at least one PTY E2E assertion for real-TTY gating and persistence
- PTY capture constraint: when engaging AltScreen, emit grep-friendly summary lines (Console layout + ConsoleCaps) via plain stderr *before* switching screen mode so `script` transcripts can assert without parsing AltScreen control codes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"IndigoRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:51:17.164232623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:14:59.640883040Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:14:59.640854226Z","close_reason":"All 23 children closed. Full frankentui console UX: startup banner, tool panels, sparklines, toasts, theme system, command palette, alt-screen split (log+timeline), capabilities detection, sensitive masking, interactive layout config, E2E tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"br-1m6a.1","title":"Rich ASCII art startup banner with full Python parity","description":"Upgrade the startup banner to be FrankenTUI-native and Python-parity-aligned.

CURRENT STATE (CODE REALITY)
- Startup banner rendering lives in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs::render_startup_banner()`.
- It already prints a strong first draft of the full experience:
  - ASCII art logo + subtitle
  - Server Configuration table
  - Database Statistics table
  - Web UI box
  - Stats Showcase JSON-ish box
  - Closing rule
- However it is still hand-built with:
  - raw ANSI escape code constants
  - manual width math / ad-hoc unicode width guesses
  - ad-hoc JSON token coloring

GOALS
- Keep rich banner enabled by default in real TTYs when `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` (default).
- Zero ANSI spam in non-TTY contexts: fall back to a single plain startup line (grep-friendly).
- Centralize styling via theme (`br-1m6a.12`) and stop scattering `\x1b[` literals through the code.
- Safety by default: all sensitive values must be masked/sanitized using `br-1m6a.18`.
- Determinism: stable output for unit tests/E2E (no time-based animations in the banner).

IMPLEMENTATION DIRECTION (PREFERRED)
- Render each banner section using real `ftui` widgets (Block/Table/Paragraph/etc) into an offscreen `ftui::Frame` at a fixed width, then export to ANSI via a FrankenTUI exporter (e.g. `ftui-extras` text exporter).
  - This eliminates fragile padding/width bookkeeping.
  - It also allows later upgrades like hyperlinks and theme variants without rewriting string math.
- Transitional allowance: it is fine if `render_startup_banner()` still returns `Vec<String>` as long as those strings are produced from widget export rather than manual ANSI.

REQUIRED SECTIONS
1) Logo header (brand)
- Maintain the ASCII art identity, but generate styling via theme rather than hardcoded CYAN/BLUE/MAG constants.
- Keep subtitle line: "Agent Coordination via Message Passing".

2) Server Configuration
- Show (at minimum):
  - environment
  - endpoint
  - database URL (sanitized)
  - storage root
  - auth enabled
  - `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED`
  - `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED`
  - `CONSOLE_THEME` (active theme id/name from `br-1m6a.12`, for discoverability)
- Values MUST pass through masking/sanitization helper.

3) Database Statistics
- Existing counts plus `pending_acks` (available in HUD snapshot).

4) Web UI
- Keep the existing Web UI box.
- `br-1m6a.9` upgrades the link set and optional OSC-8 hyperlinks.

5) JSON Stats Showcase
- `br-1m6a.17` upgrades this to real JSON + syntax highlighting.

6) Console Layout Summary (for testability)
- Include a single line describing the active console layout config (from `br-1m6a.19`).
  - E2E must be able to assert the mode without parsing inline/alt-screen control codes.

GATING
- Banner should only render when:
  - `config.log_rich_enabled == true`
  - `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
- Otherwise: emit one plain startup line like:
  - `mcp-agent-mail listening on http://HOST:PORT (rich console disabled or non-tty)`

TESTING
- Update/extend existing `console.rs` banner tests to assert stable headers/sections and that secrets are redacted.
- Tests should use shared ANSI/OSC stripping helpers from `br-1m6a.14`.

NOTES
- `br-1m6a.10` remains the focused regression bead ensuring config values are masked in the banner.","notes":"STRETCH (banner polish + FrankenTUI showcase): Console capabilities + first-run discoverability

- Add a compact `Console Capabilities` box/row in the banner that surfaces what FrankenTUI detected and what features are active:
  - truecolor / hyperlinks (OSC-8) / synchronized output / mouse
  - active `CONSOLE_THEME`
  - active layout mode summary (`inline bottom 33%` etc)
- Keep at least one plain-text line (`ConsoleCaps: ...`) so `br-1m6a.15` can assert it without parsing control codes.
- If capabilities are unknown/unreliable, prefer conservative wording and allow env overrides to disable features (e.g., force hyperlinks off).

Goal: users instantly understand why hyperlinks/animations are or aren’t showing up.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.053888754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252388170Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.1","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.053888754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.10","title":"Mask sensitive values in startup banner config display","description":"Apply the shared masking/sanitization utility (`br-1m6a.18`) to the startup banner so secrets cannot leak.

CURRENT STATE
- Banner rendering lives in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs` (`render_startup_banner`).
- It prints config-ish fields (endpoint, database_url, storage_root, auth/tool logging flags).
- It currently does NOT guarantee secrets are redacted correctly.

REQUIREMENTS
- All potentially sensitive config values rendered by the banner must be passed through the shared helper(s) from `br-1m6a.18`.
- Explicit allowlist exceptions must be respected:
  - `project_key` MUST remain visible (never masked).
  - `storage_root` MUST remain visible.

WHAT TO MASK/SANITIZE IN THE BANNER
- Anything that is an actual secret:
  - bearer tokens, JWT secrets, API keys, passwords.
- Non-obvious secret carriers:
  - `database_url` (may embed `user:pass@`): sanitize credentials in-place (preserve host/db name).
  - redis URLs (if/when displayed): same.

IMPLEMENTATION
- In the "Server Configuration" section of the banner:
  - Use `sanitize_known_value(key, value)` first.
  - If key is sensitive, replace with `<redacted>`.
  - Otherwise keep the value.
- If the banner includes any JSON blob (stats showcase) that contains sensitive keys, run `mask_json()` before pretty-printing.

TESTING (REGRESSION)
- Provide a banner render test that injects a fake secret (e.g. `bearer_token = "secret123"`) and asserts:
  - Output contains `<redacted>`
  - Output does NOT contain `secret123`
- Add a test proving `project_key` stays visible when present in JSON args.
- Add a test for `database_url` credential stripping: `postgres://u:p@h/db` -> `postgres://u:<redacted>@h/db` (exact format TBD, but password must not appear).

DONE WHEN
- Startup banner cannot leak secrets, even if config values contain embedded credentials.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.447222821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.259349320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.10","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.447222821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.10","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:49.979527948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.10","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:25:55.240437818Z","created_by":"CobaltTower","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"br-1m6a.10","author":"CobaltTower","text":"Reopened: Closed prematurely; masking helper + banner integration not implemented yet.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:26:44Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-1m6a.2","title":"Tool call start/end logging panels with syntax highlighting","description":"Upgrade per-tool-call console logging to be FrankenTUI-native and parity-aligned with the legacy Python `rich_logger.py` tool panels.

CURRENT STATE
- Tool-call start/end panels already exist in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs`:
  - `render_tool_call_start()`
  - `render_tool_call_end()`
- They are currently hand-built with raw ANSI escape codes and simplistic JSON coloring.
- `dispatch_inner("tools/call")` in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` emits these panels, but does not yet consistently respect the intended config gates.

GATING (ALL MUST PASS)
- `config.log_rich_enabled == true` (default true)
- `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
- `config.tools_log_enabled == true` (env `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED`, default true)
- `config.log_tool_calls_enabled == true` (env `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED`, default should become true via `br-1m6a.16`)

START PANEL (tool-call-start)
- Title: TOOL name + timestamp + project/agent hints.
- "Input Parameters" section:
  - Pretty JSON (2-space indent)
  - Apply masking first using `br-1m6a.18`.
  - Syntax highlight JSON using `ftui-extras` `syntax` (preferred):
    - `SyntaxHighlighter::highlight(..., "json")`
- Route output:
  - If StartupDashboard is active: write to scrollback via `dashboard_write_log()`.
  - Else: `ftui_runtime::ftui_println!()`.

END PANEL (tool-call-end)
- Rule separator color-coded by status.
- Summary line/table:
  - Tool, Project, Agent, Duration, Status
  - Query summary: total queries + total query time (from per-call tracker when instrumentation enabled)
- Duration tier styling:
  - <50ms: bright green (fast)
  - <100ms: green
  - <500ms: yellow
  - <1000ms: bright yellow
  - >=1000ms: red
- Result preview:
  - JSON pretty + syntax highlight (success)
  - Structured error object (failure)
  - Truncate BOTH args/result using `config.log_tool_calls_result_max_chars` (default 2000 via `br-1m6a.16`).

QUERY STATS
- Keep the current per-tool-call tracker approach in `lib.rs`:
  - Create a fresh `QueryTracker`, `enable()` it, set it as the active tracker for the call.
  - At end, snapshot for totals/time.
- Detailed per-table breakdown panel is handled by `br-1m6a.6`.

IMPLEMENTATION DIRECTION
- Prefer rendering panels with real `ftui` widgets into an offscreen `Frame` and exporting to ANSI (via `ftui-extras` `export::TextExporter::ansi()` once added as a dependency).
  - This removes fragile width/ANSI math.
- Keep the hot path cheap:
  - If gated off, avoid JSON cloning/pretty-printing.

TESTING
- Unit tests for gating:
  - `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false` suppresses panels.
  - `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED=false` suppresses panels.
  - `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED=false` suppresses panels.
- Unit tests for truncation using config limit.
- Unit tests verifying masking helper is applied to args/result JSON.

 {"id":"br-1m6a.20","title":"Alt-screen left split console: HUD + LogViewer log pane","description":"Implement the "left 30%" style console layout: a two-pane, AltScreen dashboard where the HUD is static on the left and a scrollable/searchable log viewer lives on the right.

WHY
- Inline mode preserves terminal scrollback, but it cannot do a true left/right HUD vs log split.
- Users explicitly want layouts like "left 30%" and want to configure this interactively.

CONFIG / INPUTS
- Uses layout config from `br-1m6a.19`:
  - `CONSOLE_SPLIT_MODE=left`
  - `CONSOLE_SPLIT_RATIO_PERCENT` (e.g., 30)

GATING
- Only engage split mode when ALL are true:
  - `config.log_rich_enabled == true`
  - `stdout.is_terminal() == true`
  - running in HTTP server mode (stdio mode cannot safely run an interactive UI loop)

CORE UI (ALTSCREEN)
- Use `ftui::ScreenMode::AltScreen` for the dashboard writer.
- Render a 2-column layout:
  1) Left pane: existing HUD (reuse `render_dashboard_frame` or a factored-out HUD widget)
  2) Right pane: a real log viewer widget (see below)

LOG VIEWER (USE FRANKENTUI WIDGET)
- Do NOT hand-roll a bespoke scroll/search UI.
- Use `ftui_widgets::log_viewer::{LogViewer, LogViewerState}`.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/log_search.rs`
- Behavior:
  - Follow mode on by default (tail -f semantics)
  - `/` opens search bar
  - `n` / `N`: next/prev match
  - `f`: filter toggle (show only matching lines)
  - Up/Down/PageUp/PageDown: scroll
  - End: jump to latest
  - `?`: help overlay

LOG ROUTING (CRITICAL)
- In AltScreen mode, `TerminalWriter::write_log()` is a no-op.
- Therefore ALL "console log lines" must be routed into an in-memory buffer that the log viewer renders.
- Proposed approach:
  - Add a `DashboardLogSink` abstraction owned by `StartupDashboard`:
    - Inline mode: sink writes to `TerminalWriter::write_log()`.
    - AltScreen mode: sink appends to a ring buffer feeding `LogViewerState`.
  - Update all call sites that currently call `dashboard_write_log()` so they work in both modes.

INTERACTION
- Read key input from `/dev/tty` (preferred) or stdin.
- Only enable interactive input in HTTP mode + TTY.
- Keybindings for layout should interoperate with `br-1m6a.19` (ratio adjust, toggle help, etc.).

PERF
- Keep last N lines (e.g., 5_000) to bound memory.
- Avoid expensive ANSI parsing on every render; store both raw and ANSI-stripped if needed.

TESTING (UNIT)
- Ratio -> column width calculation + clamp.
- Log ring buffer push/overflow.
- LogViewer state transitions for:
  - append + follow mode
  - search open/close
  - filter toggle
  - scroll bounds

E2E (HOOK INTO `br-1m6a.15`)
- Start server with `CONSOLE_SPLIT_MODE=left` + ratio.
- Assert the startup output includes the plain line `Console: left 30% (alt-screen)` (or similar) so tests don’t need to parse alt-screen control codes.

NOTES
- Default remains inline HUD + scrollback. Left split is opt-in.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:50:26.959533778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:04.744630382Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:04.744534964Z","close_reason":"Implemented alt-screen split layout: LogPane wrapper in console.rs, split_columns calculator, render_split_frame two-pane renderer.  {"id":"br-1m6a.21","title":"Command palette for console actions + layout tuning","description":"Add a FrankenTUI command palette to the rich console so users can discover and execute console actions (layout tuning, theme switching, toggles, copy links) without memorizing keybindings.

WHY
- We are adding many interactive console behaviors (layout config, log search, follow mode, filters). A command palette makes this discoverable and slick.
- FrankenTUI already ships a high-quality `CommandPalette` widget with deterministic matching modes and evidence tracking.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/command_palette_lab.rs`

SCOPE
- Implement the palette initially for AltScreen mode (`br-1m6a.20`) where we already have an event loop.
- Optional stretch: expose a minimal palette overlay in Inline mode too (only if safe).

UX
- Open palette with `Ctrl+P` (and `:` as an alternate).
- Palette shows:
  - action title
  - short hint / keybinding (if any)
  - optional category (Layout / Theme / Logs / Tools / Help)
- Palette supports:
  - prefix/substring/fuzzy matching
  - deterministic ordering (stable tests)

ACTIONS (MINIMUM)
Layout:
- Switch console mode: `inline`, `left split`
- Set split ratio: 20/30/40/50
- Increase/decrease HUD height percent (inline mode)
- Toggle anchor top/bottom
- Toggle inline auto-size
- Persist console settings now (writes `CONSOLE_*` keys to the user envfile from `br-1m6a.19`)

Theme (from `br-1m6a.12`):
- Cycle theme
- Set theme: `cyberpunk_aurora|darcula|lumen_light|nordic_frost|high_contrast`

Logs:
- Toggle follow mode (log viewer)
- Open search (`/`)
- Toggle filter-only-matches
- Clear log buffer (optional)

Tool panels (runtime toggles):
- Toggle `LOG_TOOL_CALLS_ENABLED`
- Toggle `TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED`

Help:
- Show keybindings overlay
- Show current config summary (sanitized)

PERSISTENCE / SAFETY
- Persistence is enabled by default when `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=true` (from `br-1m6a.19`).
- Writes MUST go to the user envfile:
  - default: `~/.config/mcp-agent-mail/config.env`
  - override: `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH=/path/to/file`
- MUST NOT write to repo `.env`.
- Persist ONLY `CONSOLE_*` keys (layout + `CONSOLE_THEME`). Do not silently persist unrelated server config.

IMPLEMENTATION
- Add `ConsoleCommandPalette` state struct owned by the console runtime in AltScreen mode.
- Use `ftui_widgets::command_palette::{CommandPalette, ActionItem}`.
- On action selection:
  - update in-memory console state (apply live if possible)
  - if `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=true`, persist via `mcp_agent_mail_core::config::update_envfile()`
  - show a confirmation toast (`br-1m6a.4`) or a one-line log fallback

TESTING
- Unit test action dispatch mapping (selecting action updates expected config fields).
- Unit test stable ordering of action list.
- Unit test persistence writes only `CONSOLE_*` keys.
- Render smoke test (no panic) for palette open/closed.

NOTES
- This bead is about console UX only; it must not change server semantics.","notes":"Implementation refinement (ftui demo: crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/command_palette_lab.rs)
- Use `ftui_widgets::command_palette::{CommandPalette, ActionItem, MatchFilter}` directly; avoid custom fuzzy logic.
- Enable evidence tracking in dev/test builds (`palette.enable_evidence_tracking(true)`) so we can debug ranking deterministically (and assert on evidence if needed).
- Match modes: consider cycling `MatchFilter::{All,Exact,Prefix,WordStart,Substring,Fuzzy}` via a key (like `Ctrl+M`) in AltScreen for easy tuning; keep default `Fuzzy`.
- Action IDs should be stable strings (e.g. `layout.inline`, `layout.split_left`, `logs.follow_toggle`, `tools.log_tool_calls_toggle`) so persistence/tests can target them.
- Determinism: action list ordering should be stable before matching (sort by category then title, tie-break by id) so tests do not depend on hash iteration.
- Optional but slick: expose a small "evidence ledger" side panel for the currently highlighted action (toggle with `Ctrl+E`) mirroring the demo; helpful when tuning the palette.

User-facing UX requirements
- Palette is the discoverability surface for interactive console configuration:
  - layout ratio and HUD height must be adjustable from the palette (not only hidden keybindings)
  - include "Reset layout to defaults" and "Reset theme to default" actions
- Auto-persist by default:
  - if `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=true` (default), any layout/theme-changing action should persist immediately
  - keep an explicit "Persist now" action for `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE=false`
- Keep a small confirmation toast/log line after any action that changes layout/theme:
  - include the new value (e.g. "Split ratio: 30% (saved)")

  - open palette (`Ctrl+P`), type a query (e.g. `split 30`), hit Enter
  - assert `CONSOLE_SPLIT_RATIO_PERCENT=30` written to `CONSOLE_PERSIST_PATH`
  - assert a confirmation toast/line is present (after ANSI/OSC normalization)

Test ideas
- Pure unit tests for action dispatch: selecting action id mutates the expected in-memory state + persisted envfile (only `CONSOLE_*` keys) when enabled.
- Snapshot-ish tests on stripped output: opening palette renders a stable title + at least N action rows (no exact spacing).
- If evidence ledger implemented: assert evidence entries are non-empty for a fuzzy query like `log` and ordering is stable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:23.492545480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:56.115687671Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:15:56.115647546Z","close_reason":"Implemented command palette: ConsoleCommandPalette in console.rs with 25 actions across 5 categories (Layout, Theme, Logs, Tools, Help). -{"id":"br-1m6a.22","title":"Structured event timeline view (AltScreen)","description":"Add an AltScreen-only structured event timeline view (filters + details) so Agent Mail feels like an observability cockpit, not just a wall of logs.

WHY
- Logs are useful, but a structured timeline makes it instantly clear what happened: tool calls, messages, conflicts, ack escalations, etc.
- FrankenTUI has a strong reference implementation for an event stream viewer.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/action_timeline.rs`

SCOPE
- AltScreen mode only (build on `br-1m6a.20`).
- Timeline is a *view* over the same internal events we already emit as log lines.

DATA MODEL
- Introduce a small internal `ConsoleEvent` struct (server crate) with:
  - `ts` (monotonic sequence or ISO timestamp)
  - `kind` (tool_call_start/tool_call_end/http_request/message_sent/reservation_conflict/etc)
  - `severity` (info/warn/error)
  - `summary` (short)
  - `fields: Vec<(String,String)>` (sanitized)
  - optional `json: serde_json::Value` (sanitized)

BUFFER
- Bounded ring buffer (e.g., last 500 events) stored in `StartupDashboard` so it is available to UI render.

UI
- Split right pane into:
  - Timeline list with filters (severity/kind/project/agent)
  - Detail panel showing sanitized fields + optional pretty JSON (syntax highlighted)
- Navigation:
  - Up/Down select
  - Enter toggles detail focus
  - `f` cycles filters
  - `/` search summaries
  - `?` help overlay

INTEGRATION
- Emit `ConsoleEvent` alongside existing log lines in these code paths:
  - tools/call start/end
  - request panel emission
  - send/reply/handshake tools
  - file reservation conflict detection
- Always sanitize via `br-1m6a.18` before storing.

COMMAND PALETTE
- Add a palette action (from `br-1m6a.21`) to toggle the right pane between:
  - Log Viewer
  - Event Timeline

TESTING
- Unit tests for ring buffer overflow + stable ordering.
- Unit tests for filter logic.
- Render smoke test (no panic) for empty timeline.

NOTE
- This is a polish/showcase feature (P2). It should not block core parity work.","notes":"Implementation refinement (ftui demo: crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/action_timeline.rs)
- Model the timeline as a bounded `VecDeque<ConsoleEvent>` (MAX_EVENTS ~500) plus UI state: `selected`, `scroll_offset`, `follow`, `show_details`, and filter toggles.
- Filtering should be applied as a view over the ring buffer (do not duplicate events); selection should clamp and follow mode should keep the latest selected.
- Keep event IDs monotonic (`next_id: u64`) for stable ordering and debug.
- Consider severity/kind filters cycling like the demo (single-key cycles through enums + None).
- Details panel: show `summary`, `fields`, and (if present) a pretty JSON block; syntax highlight with the active theme’s syntax theme.
- Event ingestion: emit structured events at the same points we already log; store sanitized fields only (masking + known-key sanitization).

UX integration notes
- Timeline view switching must be discoverable via the command palette (`br-1m6a.21`) and should not introduce keybinding conflicts with the LogViewer search UX.
- Keep all stored fields sanitized at ingestion time so the detail panel cannot leak secrets even if rendered verbatim.

Test ideas
- Ring buffer eviction: pushing MAX_EVENTS+N keeps newest, preserves ordering by id.
- Filter logic: each filter reduces the visible set correctly; search over summaries respects case-insensitivity.
- Follow mode: when enabled, selection moves to newest event after append; when disabled, selection stays put.
- Render smoke tests: empty timeline, one-event timeline, and narrow widths should not panic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:48.030861146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:14:26.130221178Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:14:26.130192785Z","close_reason":"Wired event timeline into server: ConsoleEventBuffer + TimelinePane in dashboard, Tab toggles right pane (Log/Timeline), command palette action, events emitted from record_request and tool call start/end, 24 new tests (20 unit + 4 integration)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:48.030861146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.751535168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.543676485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.22","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:53:53.646735640Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1m6a.23","title":"Console capabilities + discoverability (banner + help)","description":"Add a clear, FrankenTUI-native "Console Capabilities" + discoverability block so users instantly understand what terminal features are active (and why something may not be working).

WHY
- We lean hard on terminal features (AltScreen split, OSC-8 hyperlinks, mouse scroll, palette, log search). When something doesn't work, users need a single place to confirm whether it is a terminal limitation vs config.
- FrankenTUI already has strong capability detection and an evidence-ledger model.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/terminal_capabilities.rs`

SCOPE
- Rich console only (TTY + `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true`).
- Add:
  1) A small startup banner section: "Console Capabilities".
  2) The `?` help overlay (inline + AltScreen) should include the same capabilities plus the key discovery hints (`Ctrl+P` palette, `/` search in LogViewer, `?` help).
  3) A grep-friendly, stable-format one-liner for tests/debugging:
     - `ConsoleCaps: tc=1 osc8=1 mouse=0 sync=1 kitty=0 focus=0`
     - Exact numeric values may vary by terminal/CI; the *keys* and format must be stable.
- Must be enabled by default; no user action required.

IMPLEMENTATION
- Introduce a small `ConsoleCaps` struct in the server crate, computed once at startup from FrankenTUI/terminal capability detection.
- Use the caps to gate behavior where appropriate:
  - OSC-8 hyperlinks: only emit when `osc8=1` (still always include raw URL text).
  - Mouse-only UX (hover tooltips, scroll targeting): only enable when `mouse=1`.
- Degrade gracefully:
  - Non-TTY or `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=false`: do not emit capability panels; keep output plain/grep-friendly.

TESTING
- Unit tests:
  - formatting of `ConsoleCaps:` line is ASCII-only and stable.
  - caps-to-string never panics on "unknown" values.
- Render/integration tests:
  - banner contains "Console Capabilities" header after ANSI/OSC normalization.
- E2E (`br-1m6a.15`):
  - in a PTY run, assert `ConsoleCaps:` line exists (do not assert exact values; assert presence of all keys).

NOTES
- If FrankenTUI exposes an evidence ledger for caps, optionally show a compact "why" hint in the help overlay (not required for MVP).","notes":"E2E constraint (PTY capture)
- The `ConsoleCaps:` one-liner needs to be emitted via a guaranteed plain-text path (stderr log line) so `script` PTY transcripts capture it even when the console switches into AltScreen.
- Same for the split/layout summary line used by br-1m6a.15: emit it *before* entering AltScreen.

Implementation hint
- Prefer a single early emission like:
  - `Console: split=left ratio=30% (alt-screen)`
  - `ConsoleCaps: tc=1 osc8=1 mouse=0 sync=1 kitty=0 focus=0`
  emitted right after capability detection and before creating the AltScreen writer.

- br-1m6a.15 should assert presence of all ConsoleCaps keys (not values) in the normalized transcript.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkMoose","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:12:08.014743363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:06:32.000976724Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:06:32.000944935Z","close_reason":"Implemented via commit 1eda13e (ConsoleCaps + banner + help overlay); closing bead status.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.23","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:12:08.014743363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.3","title":"Sparkline throughput trends in live HUD","description":"Improve the live HUD sparkline(s) to show real, intuitive throughput trends (and showcase FrankenTUI sparkline rendering).

CURRENT STATE
- A request sparkline already exists:
  - `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs::SparklineBuffer`
  - Sampled every ~1200ms by `StartupDashboard::spawn_refresh_worker()`
  - Rendered in `render_dashboard_frame()` using `ftui::widgets::sparkline::Sparkline::render_to_string()`.
- The HUD currently labels the sparkline as `req/s` even though it is actually `req/tick` (approx).

TARGET STATE

 {"id":"br-1m6a.4","title":"Toast notifications for important agent events","description":"Add toast notifications for important agent events, using FrankenTUI's real notification queue widgets.

WHY
- Agent Mail is event-heavy (registering agents, conflicts, releases, ack escalations). A HUD without toasts forces users to scan scrollback.
- FrankenTUI already ships a polished notification stack with priority ordering, dedup, actions, and positioning.
  - Demo reference: `/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/notifications.rs`

GOALS
- Toasts are enabled by default when rich console is active in a real TTY.
- Non-HUD / non-TTY contexts must stay grep-friendly: degrade to a plain log line (no ANSI art dumps).
- Keep behavior testable without sleeps.

IMPLEMENTATION
1) Use FrankenTUI queue types
- Add to `StartupDashboard`:
  - `notifications: Mutex<ftui_widgets::notification_queue::NotificationQueue>`
- Queue config (initial):
  - `max_visible`: 3-4
  - `max_queued`: 20
  - position: `ToastPosition::TopRight` (or inside HUD area if we restrict drawing to the HUD rect)

2) API surface
- `fn push_toast(&self, toast: Toast, prio: NotificationPriority)`
- Convenience helpers:
  - `toast_info(msg)`, `toast_warning(msg)`, `toast_error(msg)`

3) Rendering
- In the dashboard render path, render `NotificationStack::new(&queue)` as an overlay.
  - If overlaying over the whole terminal is not safe in Inline mode, render it inside the HUD area only.

4) Non-HUD degradation
- If no dashboard is active (stdio mode or non-TTY), toast events become plain one-line logs.
  - Prefer `console_info/console_warning/console_error` once `br-1m6a.5` exists.
  - Otherwise: `tracing::info!/warn!/error!` or `ftui_runtime::ftui_println!()` with plain text.

EVENT HOOKS (INITIAL SET)
- In tool dispatch/instrumentation paths, trigger toasts for:
  - `register_agent` success: "Agent {name} registered ({program})"
  - `file_reservation_paths`: conflict -> warning toast with conflict summary
  - `force_release_file_reservation` success -> warning toast
  - repeated server errors: if >5 errors in 10s -> error toast
  - messaging bursts: if >20 send/reply in 5s -> info toast

ACTIONS (OPTIONAL, NICE-TO-HAVE)
- For some toasts, include actions:
  - "Copy endpoint" (copies URL to clipboard if available; otherwise just logs)
  - "Open web UI" (only if we ever support shell-out; otherwise omit)

TESTING
- Unit tests for queue behavior (no sleeps):
  - push respects max_queued
  - urgent priority jumps ahead
  - dismiss/dismiss_all behavior
  - auto-expiry can be simulated by mutating `toast.state.created_at` to an old instant, then calling `queue.tick(...)` (no wall-clock sleeps)
- Render smoke test: overlay render does not panic for empty/1/4 visible toasts.

NOTE
- If time-based animations make tests flaky, disable animations for our toasts (use reduced motion config) and keep assertions on structure/content only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.360557085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.256372808Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.4","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:00.360557085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"br-hk3v","title":"Dev UX: am runner script + /api<->/mcp HTTP path alias","description":"## Problem
Running the local HTTP server currently requires multiple manual exports (bearer token from a separate envfile, HTTP_PATH override, LOG_RICH_ENABLED) and is error-prone. In this environment some clients expect /mcp/ while the server defaults to /api/.

## Goals
- One easy entrypoint: am serve starts the server with sane defaults and rich console enabled by default.
- Auto-load HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN from a dev envfile (default: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env) if not already set.
- Support BOTH /api/ and /mcp/ for the MCP HTTP endpoint (alias each other so either client works).
- Keep backward compatibility for existing E2E scripts that hardcode /api/.

## Implementation
1) Add scripts/am shell wrapper:
- am serve (default) runs: cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765
- Defaults: HTTP_PATH=/mcp/ and LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true
- Options: --path mcp|api, --env-file <path>, --host, --port, --no-auth
- If HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN is unset, attempt to read it from env file (strip quotes).

2) Server: accept /api and /mcp as synonyms for the MCP base path:
- If configured base is /api, also accept requests under /mcp by rewriting the request path to the configured base before passing to HttpRequestHandler (configured with base_path=config.http_path).
- If configured base is /mcp, also accept /api similarly.
- Do not change behavior for arbitrary nested bases (for example /api/mcp).

3) Docs: update README.md with scripts/am usage and the /api + /mcp alias note.

## Tests
- Unit tests for the alias path logic (path_allowed + canonicalization).

## Acceptance Criteria
